---
title: "GO:BP and KEGG transcriptomics"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# install packages

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(tidyverse)
library(enrichplot)
library(patchwork)


# Separate into up- and down-regulated in Old
# Genes higher in OLD ovaries (negative log2FoldChange)
upregulated_old <- deseq_results %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange < -1)

# Genes higher in YOUNG ovaries (positive log2FoldChange)
upregulated_young <- deseq_results %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 1)

cat("Upregulated in Old:", nrow(upregulated_old), "genes\n")
cat("Upregulated in Young:", nrow(upregulated_young), "genes\n")

#Convert Ensembl IDs to Entrez IDs
entrez_up <- mapIds(org.Mm.eg.db,
                     keys = upregulated_old$ensembl_gene_id,
                     column = "ENTREZID",
                     keytype = "ENSEMBL",
                     multiVals = "first")
entrez_up <- entrez_up[!is.na(entrez_up)]

entrez_down <- mapIds(org.Mm.eg.db,
                       keys = upregulated_young$ensembl_gene_id,
                       column = "ENTREZID",
                       keytype = "ENSEMBL",
                       multiVals = "first")
entrez_down <- entrez_down[!is.na(entrez_down)]

# GO enrichment (Biological Process) 
go_up <- enrichGO(gene = entrez_up,
                   OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
                   ont = "BP",
                   pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                   qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                   readable = TRUE)

go_down <- enrichGO(gene = entrez_down,
                     OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
                     ont = "BP",
                     pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                     qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                     readable = TRUE)

#  KEGG pathway enrichment
kegg_up <- enrichKEGG(gene = entrez_up,
                       organism = "mmu",
                       pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                       qvalueCutoff = 0.05)
kegg_up <- setReadable(kegg_up, OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID")

kegg_down <- enrichKEGG(gene = entrez_down,
                         organism = "mmu",
                         pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
                         qvalueCutoff = 0.05)
kegg_down <- setReadable(kegg_down, OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID")

# Shared theme so GO and KEGG panels match exactly
theme_compare <- theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")  # ← increased size, added bold
  )
direction_colours <- c("Increased in Old" = "#D73027",
                        "Decreased in Old (Higher in Young)" = "#4575B4")

# Helper to build a comparison data frame from a pair of enrichResults
build_comparison <- function(res_up, res_down, n = 10, wrap_width = 50) {
  if (nrow(res_up@result) == 0 | nrow(res_down@result) == 0) {
    warning("One of the enrichment results has no significant terms; skipping this comparison.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  up_data <- res_up@result[1:min(n, nrow(res_up@result)), ]
  up_data$Direction <- "Increased in Old"
  up_data$neglog10p <- -log10(up_data$p.adjust)

  down_data <- res_down@result[1:min(n, nrow(res_down@result)), ]
  down_data$Direction <- "Decreased in Old (Higher in Young)"
  down_data$neglog10p <- -log10(down_data$p.adjust)

  combined <- rbind(
    up_data[, c("Description", "Count", "p.adjust", "Direction", "neglog10p")],
    down_data[, c("Description", "Count", "p.adjust", "Direction", "neglog10p")]
  )

  # Wrap long descriptions so axis labels stay readable
  combined$Description <- str_wrap(combined$Description, width = wrap_width)
  combined
}

# GO comparison barplot
go_combined <- build_comparison(go_up, go_down)
go_combined$Description <- gsub(
  "adaptive immune response based on somatic\nrecombination of immune receptors built from\nimmunoglobulin superfamily domains",
  "somatic recombination of immune receptors",
  go_combined$Description
)

p_go <- ggplot(go_combined, aes(x = reorder(Description, neglog10p), y = neglog10p, fill = Direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = direction_colours) +
  labs(x = "", y = "-log10(adjusted p-value)", title = "GO Biological Process") +
  theme_compare

# KEGG comparison barplot 
kegg_combined <- build_comparison(kegg_up, kegg_down)

p_kegg <- ggplot(kegg_combined, aes(x = reorder(Description, neglog10p), y = neglog10p, fill = Direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = direction_colours) +
  labs(x = "", y = "-log10(adjusted p-value)", title = "KEGG Pathway") +
  theme_compare

# Combine into one patchwork figure with a single shared legend 
final_figure <- (p_go / p_kegg) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Old vs Young Ovaries: Divergent Biological Processes and Pathways",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 18))
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16)
  )

final_figure

ggsave("GO_KEGG_old_vs_young_patchwork.pdf", final_figure, width = 14, height = 18)
```
